chief_complaint: A 61-year-old male with prostate cancer developed swelling and pain in his right lower limb, with localized warmth and worsening symptoms upon activity, one week after discharge from robotic-assisted laparoscopic radical prostatectomy.
history: The patient underwent RALRP for prostate cancer (pT3N0M0) and was initially managed symptomatically for a suspected pelvic lymphocyst. However, three weeks later, he returned with persistent right lower limb edema and new-onset dyspnea.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 37.2°C, blood pressure 120/80 mmHg, pulse 90 bpm, respiratory rate 20 breaths/min. Physical examination revealed localized warmth and swelling in the right lower limb.
labs: Further evaluation confirmed a secondary PE due to the lymphocyst. The patient showed significant improvement after a comprehensive treatment strategy, including anticoagulation, thrombolysis, and inferior vena cava filter placement.
correct_diagnosis: Pulmonary Embolism secondary to lymphocyst after Robot-Assisted Laparoscopic Radical Prostatectomy
differentials: Pulmonary Embolism secondary to lymphocyst after Robot-Assisted Laparoscopic Radical Prostatectomy
correct_ranking: Pulmonary Embolism secondary to lymphocyst after Robot-Assisted Laparoscopic Radical Prostatectomy
